race.name: Thorleif Haugs Minneløp
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers through the Finnemarka forests honoring the man who won three gold medals at the first Winter Olympics — where Norway's skiing heritage runs deepest.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations through the Finnemarka forest circuit

climate.description: February in the Finnemarka forests west of Oslo. At moderate altitude, conditions are typically cold but less extreme than mountain races. Temperatures -10°C to -2°C. Snow reliability is generally good but proximity to the milder Oslo-region climate means occasional thaw risk.
challenges: Snow reliability less certain than mountain venues
Forest terrain can be icy in shaded sections
Hilly course demands technical classic skiing
February thaw risk in warmer years

course.primary: Forest circuit through the Finnemarka wilderness area west of Drammen. Rolling, hilly terrain through dense Norwegian forest. The course features genuine elevation changes with climbs and technical descents on classic tracks. Named after Thorleif Haug, who dominated the 1924 Chamonix Olympics.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through hilly forest terrain
course.grooming: Good quality, Ski Classics Challengers standard
features: Hilly Finnemarka forest — genuine elevation challenges
Named for Norway's first Winter Olympic triple gold medalist
Technical descents on classic tracks through forest
Accessible from Oslo/Drammen — one of the closest marathons to the capital

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Prestige boosted by 60-year history honoring Thorleif Haug — one of skiing's most iconic figures. Technical forest course rewards skilled classic skiers. Good accessibility from Oslo region. Snow reliability is the primary weakness.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score 61 >= 60 qualifies for TIER 2. Prestige 4 — already T2, cannot promote into T1.

history.summary: Founded in 1966 to honor Thorleif Haug (1894–1934), who won three gold medals in cross-country skiing and Nordic combined at the inaugural 1924 Winter Olympics in Chamonix. The 50km classic race through the Finnemarka forests near his home region has become one of Norway's most respected memorial ski marathons. Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series.
notable_facts: Honors Thorleif Haug — triple gold medalist at the 1924 Chamonix Olympics
Haug won the 18km, 50km, and Nordic combined at the first Winter Games
Founded in 1966, over 60 years of continuous history
Finnemarka is a popular wilderness area for Oslo-region skiers
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
One of the most technically demanding forest courses in Norway
